tlos: TLO 1. Demonstrate necessary skills as satellite system operators and crew commanders and gain exposure to satellite operations and data collection tasks.
TLO 2. Recall tenants of nominal and anomalous satellite operations.

events.description: An overview of the GROOT system and its operation are provided.

Maneuver academics introduces the student to mission planning, briefing, execution, and debrief (PBED) of two satellites operating near each other to students with limited satellite operations and rendezvous and proximity operations (RPO) experience.
Robotics academics reinforces PBED and continues to build basic satellite ops knowledge and skills.
The profiles for this simulator event are structured to familiarize students with manual and automatic translation and rotation of the GROOT vehicle.

The profiles for this simulator event are structured to familiarize students with the various operational modes of the robotic arm, to demonstrate successful grapple, and to demonstrate various impedances to successful grapple.

events.eventName: GROOT Supervised Exploration and Check-ride Prep SO-1
events.description: These simulator sessions are structured to allow students to explore the GROOT simulator, develop proficiency with CRM, maneuver, and robotics operations. Students will use these sessions to develop their check-ride profiles.
events.rangeInfo: Part of SO 6130-2S series (SO-1 through SO-3)

events.eventName: GROOT Supervised Exploration and Check-ride Prep SO-2
events.description: These simulator sessions are structured to allow students to explore the GROOT simulator, develop proficiency with CRM, maneuver, and robotics operations. Students will use these sessions to develop their check-ride profiles.
events.rangeInfo: Part of SO 6130-2S series (SO-1 through SO-3)

events.eventName: GROOT Supervised Exploration and Check-ride Prep SO-3
events.description: These simulator sessions are structured to allow students to explore the GROOT simulator, develop proficiency with CRM, maneuver, and robotics operations. Students will use these sessions to develop their check-ride profiles.
events.rangeInfo: Part of SO 6130-2S series (SO-1 through SO-3)

events.description: Students will demonstrate proficiency on manual and automatic maneuver and robotic operations.

prerequisites.name: GROOT Supervised Exploration and Check-ride Prep SO-3

tlos: TLO 1. Demonstrate skills as satellite system operators and crew commanders and gain exposure to satellite operations and data collection tasks.
TLO 2. Recall tenants of nominal and anomalous satellite operations.

events.description: Students are introduced to roles and responsibilities, battle rhythm, flight safety and best practices for Aerocube satellite operations.

Students are introduced to the Aerocube space segment, satellite modes, vehicle commanding, telemetry, orbit, and attitude. Students are also provided an overview of the Aerocube ground segment, stations and tools

This event is designed to introduce students to the display layout for Aerocube operations. Students will learn how to configure the operator and crew commander terminals and discuss how each element supports operations.

Students will complete an examination to configure the display and explain operational relevance of each window. Scheduled concurrently with SO 6231O Contact Demo. Students must pass their exam to proceed into the contact demo

prerequisites.name: Aerocube Display Familiarization
prerequisites.notes: SO 6220H listed as 'SOCR Display Familiarization' in event definition

events.description: Students will learn to build commands, pass plans, and how to run a nominal Aerocube satellite contact. Scheduled concurrently with SO 6230E SOCR Display Exam.

prerequisites.name: SOCR Display Familiarization
prerequisites.notes: SO 6220H listed as 'Aerocube Display Familiarization' in prerequisites section

events.description: This event is designed to allow students to serve as operator and then crew commander on a live satellite contact. Students will perform nominal crew operations on Aerocube without supervising instructor input.

This event is designed to allow students to serve as operator and then crew commander on a live satellite contact. Students will perform nominal crew operations on Aerocube without supervising instructor input.
